channel.title: maGaming RSS Feed
channel.description: A cleaned-up version of the original gaming feed

item.title: Pragmata on Switch 2 offers superb sci-fi with a few hiccups
item.content:encoded:
Outside of Nintendo’s own development teams, there are few companies that have managed to make the Switch 2 sing in quite the same way as Capcom. While others are still dipping a toe in with remasters and ports of older titles, Capcom has committed to bringing their latest flagship games to the Switch 2, day and date with other platforms. After their stunning success bringing Resident Evil Requiem across, hopes are high for a similar story with Pragmata, a brand-new sci-fi IP that leans on the same in-house RE Engine technology.

Pragmata’s performance on Switch 2 is immediately interesting, as Capcom are once again pushing for a 60fps frame rate target. In order to reach that goal, they’ve obviously had to make various graphical compromises, from lowering effects quality, to texture detail and the internal resolution.

One of the most noticeable aspects is with the clearly downgraded shadow maps, which are incredibly basic compared to the character models they’re attempting to replicate, and which move wholly unrealistically across central character Hugh’s space suit when he’s just standing about. You also have things like Diana’s more rigid hair, simplified lighting, reduced reflections in the environments often very shiny surfaces, and more.

What you do have in terms of image settings are HDR, Color Space and the option to alter lens distortion and chromatic aberration, but that’s your lot. Lens Distortion and Chromatic Aberration are the most interesting elements here, and turning them both off results in a cleaner, more defined image, especially when upscaling to a 4K resolution.

However, the overall lighting effects with both of these enabled feels better and, to my mind, enhances the atmosphere to the extent that I stuck with them enabled. In turn, performance also felt just that little more reliable with both on, and there’s clearly some interplay between DLSS upscaling and the two visual options that goes hand in hand. I’d assume that since they’re both turned on as standard, this is how Capcom expect you to experience the game.

Pragmata looks good on Switch 2, but in motion the shadows are distracting
On PS5, the shadows are higher quality, reflections extend further etc.
These scenes show the worst of Switch 2’s lighting and card-based hair
PS5 just has much deeper and more nuanced light, shadow and strand-based hair

As with other Switch 2 releases and Pragmata’s RE Engine brethren, hair is one of the key visual features that has to be turned down to ensure a stable frame rate. Diana has a great mop of long hair, and the Switch 2 version trades in the strand-based physics rendering for more rigid cards, though I personally found this slightly easier to live with than the frizz of Resident Evil Requiem’s Grace, as the overall visual design makes the transition somewhat less noticeable. There are times outside of cutscenes where it’s obvious that the facial details are similarly turned down, but in the heat of the moment, you won’t notice.

Pragmata’s central gameplay relies on hacking, shooting and dodging, and there’s a really interesting interplay between the three. It carries a lot of weight, some of that feeling true to Hugh, his space suit, and the small android girl riding on this back. Crucially, it looks and feels good on Switch 2, and if you’re not playing elsewhere, then I don’t think you’d give it a second thought.

Despite all of these tradeoffs, Switch 2 cannot hold onto a fixed 60fps and is instead more variable – as already seen with the Sketchbook demo released earlier this year. This is, of course, much more of an issue when playing docked where VRR isn’t possible on Switch 2, while handheld play does have VRR and can compensate for some of this variability. In both scenarios I found it remained very playable and enjoyable, thanks to the pace of the moment-to-moment gunplay and hacking mechanics, though it does feel more cramped on the smaller screen and I had a few problems handling Pragmata’s screen-filling bosses. If you can, this is a game best experienced on the big screen, and in that case, you might want to consider other platforms.

There’s still questions over why Capcom don’t offer the option to target 30fps on Switch 2 for a more consistent experience. The lowest ebbs of the frame rates affected their Switch 2 Resident Evil trio to varying degrees, and I feel that with Pragmata it could really have helped with the distracting shadows here, even if the base resolution remained similar. We expect it remains in the region of 360p handheld and 540p docked, as was seen with the Sketchbook demo.

Switch 2 absolutely holds it own, though, and just as with Resident Evil Requiem, you’re getting the full-fat experience on a handheld console. At no point have I felt hard done by playing the game here, and my colleagues who’ve been checking out the PS5 version can’t walk out the door with it. Well, not without getting some funny looks. The RE Engine continues to offer far better scalability across platforms than Unreal Engine does, and it only serves to enhance Capcom’s impressive run of top-tier games.

Pragmata itself is massively enjoyable, and as a new IP, there’s very few games out there that feel as assured or fully realised. Hugh is looking to escape from a moon base that is disintegrating around him, with the AI and its robotic minions thoroughly set on removing their uninvited guest. Diana, meanwhile, is delightful and precocious, and Hugh’s relationship with her is almost immediately protective and parental. It forms the backbone of the game, and thankfully, they’re protagonists that you’ll want to guide through to the end. Tie that into brilliant combat, gently Souls-like exploration and an intriguing world, and you’ve got a sci-fi game that pushes the action-adventure genre forward in some really interesting ways.

Pragmata is another Capcom hit, brought to the Switch 2 in enjoyable, if not quite perfect, form. The engaging combat, the compelling setting and the steadily growing relationship between Hugh and Diana is one of the highlights of 2026.



https://www.thesixthaxis.com/2026/04/16/pragmata-switch-2-review/
item.dc:creator: invalid@example.com (ResetEra_Newsbot

item.title: PRAGMATA: The Hacking Reset Secret Technique to Improve Combat
item.content:encoded:
Hacking is a core component of the PRAGMATA combat mechanics and the most difficult to master. As you juggle between hacking and shooting enemies, you may find hacking cumbersome and distracting, but there is a hidden input trick that completely eliminates hacking downtime. Mastering this "Hacking Reset" tech not only allows you to hack more consistently but also lets you build the Hacking Gauge faster to access powerful abilities more often.

Table of contents

  • Why Your Hacking Might Feel Slow
  • How to Perform the Hacking Reset Tech
  • Why You Need This: Faster Overdrive and Auto-Hacking

Why Your Hacking Might Feel Slow

A common source of frustration in the first few hours of the game is how hacking and shooting don't seem to mesh well. Most players naturally hold down the L2/LT/ZL (Aim) button during combat encounters, letting it go only when they require faster mobility. However, holding the aim button prevents the Hacking Matrix from reappearing automatically.

As such, by holding your aim throughout a fight, you are actually limiting your most valuable combat asset, making combat feel much slower and clunkier than it is.

How to Perform the Hacking Reset Tech

The key to taking your PRAGMATA gameplay to the next level is treating the L2/LT/ZL button like a rhythmic pump rather than a static toggle. This Reset Trick bypasses downtime, enabling fast hacking cycles.

How to execute the Hacking Reset:

  1. Hold L2/LT to bring up the Matrix and complete your hack.
  2. Release L2/LT immediately upon a successful hack
  3. Instantly hold L2/LT again during the slowed time frame following a successful hack to force the Hacking Matrix to pop up again with no delay.

By pumping the trigger this way, you remove any frustration from the combat loop. This allows you to stack status effects and hacking damage on enemies in a fraction of the time it takes using the standard method.

While the damage difference between this tech and traditional weakpoint shooting isn't massive without builds that prioritize Hacking damage, the true value lies in simplifying your mental stack. By focusing entirely on the hacking rhythm, you remove the need to precisely aim at weakpoints (which are sometimes placed on enemies' sides or backs), allowing you to manage crowded encounters with much less stress.

Why You Need This: Faster Overdrive and Auto-Hacking

Mastering this Hacking Reset tech comes with some additional benefits that will further improve your gameplay.

  • Since you are completing multiple hacks in a short time, your Hacking Gauge will fill up significantly faster. This lets you trigger Overdrive Mode way more often
  • As it fills the Hacking Gauge much faster, this Reset Trick also enables more uses of Auto-Hacking, which requires a portion of the gauge to execute.
  • When Offense Mode is active, hacks deal increased damage to enemies that have been opened. The Hacking Reset Tech turns your hacking into a weapon with a high rate of fire that can melt health bars without using physical ammo.
  • As you are using Hacking as your primary damage dealer, you also have more chances to activate Critical Shot nodes for increased Lunafilament gain.

This concludes our guide. For more help, visit our PRAGMATA Complete Walkthrough and Guide hub.

Guide based on a full playthrough completed at Standard difficulty in the PC version of the game. Screenshots captured from the same version.

About the author: Francesco De Meo has been covering video games and technology since 2012, starting his career at small outlets like Gamersyndrome and GeekSnack. After joining Wccftech gaming section in 2015, he quickly expanded his video gaming coverage with in-depth reporting, interviews with iconic industry figures such as Grasshopper Manufacture founder and No More Heroes creator Goichi "Suda51" Suda, Resident Evil series creator Shinji Mikami, Team NINJA's president and Nioh series director Fumihiko Yasuda, and Silent Hill creator Keiichiro Toyama, reviews and on-the-ground coverage of major industry events such as Gamescom and E3. When he's not reporting or reviewing, Francesco can be found playing the genres he loves most, spending time with his six cats, reading, writing music, playing guitar and drumming for his progressive rock band.

Follow Wccftech on Google to get more of our news coverage in your feeds.



https://wccftech.com/how-to/pragmata-the-hacking-reset-secret-technique-to-optimize-combat-flow/
item.dc:creator: invalid@example.com (ResetEra_Newsbot

item.title: PRAGMATA: All Mr. Cabin Stamp Rewards – Spend Your Cabin Coins Wisely
item.content:encoded:
Unlike some of the other currencies, Cabin Coins in PRAGMATA are a limited resource. You will only find these by thoroughly scouring sectors or mastering the optional objectives in Training Simulations like Bad Blood or Floor It.

Because you never find these coins in droves, you cannot afford to spend them blindly. The Mr. Cabin Stamp Card hides many of its rewards, and while some unlock vital gameplay enhancements, others are purely cosmetic 3D models. To help you avoid wasting your rare coins, we have listed all rewards below.

NOTE: The guide currently lists all the rewards of the Trainee and Associate Card. The rewards for other stamp cards will be added in a future update.

Table of contents

  • How the Mr. Cabin Stamp System Works
  • Every Mr. Cabin Stamp Reward
    • Trainee Card
    • Associate Card

How the Mr. Cabin Stamp System Works

Once you find a Cabin Coin, you can stamp one of the cards by speaking with Mr. Cabin in the Shelter. The rewards are laid out in a grid, and most are hidden behind "???" icons until you spend a coin to reveal the reward.

This is where the risk lies. If you don't engage with the side content, you could easily waste your limited coins on a bot model instead of a critical combat buff.

Every Mr. Cabin Stamp Reward

Rewards are listed from left to right, starting from the top line.

Trainee Card

  • Watcher Bot Data / Sector Guard Bot Data / Overclocked Weaponry Mod
  • Skirmisher Mod / Walker Bot Data / Self Defense Response Mod
  • Crusher Bot Data / Red Gate Key / Hugh's Exolayer Outfit
  • Bingo: Cartdrigde Holder
  • Triple Bingo: Freeze Hacking Node
  • Blackout (Clearing the reward card): Diana's Lunar Aqua Outfit

Associate Card

  • Quick Fix Mod / Executor Bot Data / Secret Report 02 / Sweeper Bot
  • Spider Bot Data / Secret Report 01 / Reassembler Bot Data / Missile Watcher Bot Data
  • Hyperfocus Mod / Creator Bot Data / Performance Boost Mod / Red Gate Key
  • Secret Report 03 / Optimal Performance Mod / Hugh's Exolayer Type B Outfit / Cartridge Holder
  • Bingo: Expose Hacking Node
  • Triple Bingo: Drone Hive Defense Unit
  • Blackout (Clearing the reward car): Hugh's Legion Zero outfit

Which Rewards Should You Prioritize?

If you aren't planning to 100% the game, you should prioritize the following rewards:

  1. Any reward that unlocks new combat and build options - Mods and Cardridge Holders
  2. Red Gate Keys: Used to open Red Gate Zones, which reward you with unique rewards upon completing and unlock sealed sections of Stamp Cards
  3. Secret Reports, which add some backstory details
  4. Any cosmetic and collectible

This concludes our guide. For more help, visit our PRAGMATA Complete Walkthrough and Guide hub.

Guide based on a full playthrough completed at Standard difficulty in the PC version of the game. Screenshots captured from the same version

About the author: Francesco De Meo has been covering video games and technology since 2012, starting his career at small outlets like Gamersyndrome and GeekSnack. After joining Wccftech gaming section in 2015, he quickly expanded his video gaming coverage with in-depth reporting, interviews with iconic industry figures such as Grasshopper Manufacture founder and No More Heroes creator Goichi "Suda51" Suda, Resident Evil series creator Shinji Mikami, Team NINJA's president and Nioh series director Fumihiko Yasuda, and Silent Hill creator Keiichiro Toyama, reviews and on-the-ground coverage of major industry events such as Gamescom and E3. When he's not reporting or reviewing, Francesco can be found playing the genres he loves most, spending time with his six cats, reading, writing music, playing guitar and drumming for his progressive rock band.

Follow Wccftech on Google to get more of our news coverage in your feeds.



https://wccftech.com/how-to/pragmata-all-mr-cabin-stamp-rewards-spend-your-cabin-coins-wisely/
item.dc:creator: invalid@example.com (ResetEra_Newsbot

item.title: PRAGMATA: Mouse Trap Training Simulation Guide (Defeat All Enemies Within The Recycler)
item.content:encoded:
Among PRAGMATA's optional Training Simulations, there are some of them that can be difficult to complete while clearing all of the optional objectives. Besides the Medic Training Simulation, the Mouse Trap simulation can be challenging to clear fully, as you will need to be patient and make full use of the Decoy Gun.

Table of contents

  • Simulation Objectives & Rewards
  • Defeat All Enemies Within The Recyler + Complete Within 50 Seconds
    • Step-by-Step Strategy

Simulation Objectives & Rewards

NOTE: For any replay of any training mission, you can get 50 Lunafilament for clearing objectives you already cleared in a previous run.

Defeat All Enemies Within The Recyler + Complete Within 50 Seconds

While completing the Mouse Trap training mission by clearing enemies is relatively simple, clearing the Defeat all Enemies with the Recycler and Complete Within 50 secs objectives is trickier. The Recycler can only be used once, so you need to make absolutely sure every bot that spawns around the arena gets inside it before activating.

This can be achieved fairly easily by following the strategy below.

Step-by-Step Strategy

  1. Collect the Decoy Gun
  2. Don't fire your Decoy just yet! Get in the middle of the recycler and wait about 10 seconds for the bots to reach you. You may have to dodge a few of their attacks. If you are getting damaged, it's not a big deal.
  3. After the ten seconds have passed, fire the Decoy gun to make sure the bots don't leave the Recycler.
  4. Activate it by completing the activation puzzle.
  5. This will instantly destroy every bot, clearing both optional objectives.

This concludes our guide. For more help, visit our PRAGMATA Complete Walkthrough and Guide hub.

Guide based on a full playthrough completed at Standard difficulty in the PC version of the game. Screenshots captured from the same version

About the author: Francesco De Meo has been covering video games and technology since 2012, starting his career at small outlets like Gamersyndrome and GeekSnack. After joining Wccftech gaming section in 2015, he quickly expanded his video gaming coverage with in-depth reporting, interviews with iconic industry figures such as Grasshopper Manufacture founder and No More Heroes creator Goichi "Suda51" Suda, Resident Evil series creator Shinji Mikami, Team NINJA's president and Nioh series director Fumihiko Yasuda, and Silent Hill creator Keiichiro Toyama, reviews and on-the-ground coverage of major industry events such as Gamescom and E3. When he's not reporting or reviewing, Francesco can be found playing the genres he loves most, spending time with his six cats, reading, writing music, playing guitar and drumming for his progressive rock band.

Follow Wccftech on Google to get more of our news coverage in your feeds.



https://wccftech.com/how-to/pragmata-mouse-trap-training-simulation-guide-defeat-all-enemies-within-the-recycler/
item.dc:creator: invalid@example.com (ResetEra_Newsbot

item.title: PRAGMATA: Bad Blood Training Simulation Guide (Defeat 4 Enemies With Friendly Fire)
item.content:encoded:
PRAGMATA's Training Simulations, such as Medic Training, Mouse Trap, and Floor It, feel more like puzzles, forcing you to take full advantage of the game's mechanics to clear them with all optional objectives.

This is also the case of the Bad Blood Training Simulation, where you need to use hacking mechanics to your advantage to perfect it.

Table of contents

  • Simulation Objectives & Rewards
  • Defeat 4 Enemies With Friendly Fire + Complete Within 100 Seconds
    • Step-by-Step Strategy

Simulation Objectives & Rewards

Defeat 4 Enemies With Friendly Fire + Complete Within 100 Seconds

To clear these objectives, you need to leverage the synergy between the Multihack and Confuse hacking nodes. Any time you go through a Multihack node, every other effect activated during that hacking session will apply to any nearby enemy, including Confusion. This effectively lets you pit multiple bots against one another.

Step-by-Step Strategy

  1. At the start of the simulation, wait for every enemy to spawn while you acquire both Multihack and Confuse.
  2. Target the Impeder, and hack it by passing through a Multihack and a Confuse node. This will make every enemy attack one another.
  3. Don't wait for them to destroy one another, however. Attack a few of them, otherwise you will not be able to clear both waves of bots within 100 seconds.
  4. Repeat the same when the second wave spawns. Hack to open enemies and attack, but don't waste multihack or confuse if you don't need to activate the effects again - you only have four total uses.
  5. Keep damaging the weaker bots until they are at critical health. This will make it easier for the more powerful bots to dispose of them to clear the Defeat 4 enemies with friendly fire.

This concludes our guide. For more help, visit our PRAGMATA Complete Walkthrough and Guide hub.

Guide based on a full playthrough completed at Standard difficulty in the PC version of the game. Screenshots captured from the same version

About the author: Francesco De Meo has been covering video games and technology since 2012, starting his career at small outlets like Gamersyndrome and GeekSnack. After joining Wccftech gaming section in 2015, he quickly expanded his video gaming coverage with in-depth reporting, interviews with iconic industry figures such as Grasshopper Manufacture founder and No More Heroes creator Goichi "Suda51" Suda, Resident Evil series creator Shinji Mikami, Team NINJA's president and Nioh series director Fumihiko Yasuda, and Silent Hill creator Keiichiro Toyama, reviews and on-the-ground coverage of major industry events such as Gamescom and E3. When he's not reporting or reviewing, Francesco can be found playing the genres he loves most, spending time with his six cats, reading, writing music, playing guitar and drumming for his progressive rock band.

Follow Wccftech on Google to get more of our news coverage in your feeds.



https://wccftech.com/how-to/pragmata-bad-blood-training-simulation-guide-defeat-4-enemies-with-friendly-fire/

item.title: PRAGMATA: Floor It Training Simulation Guide (Complete Without Taking Damage)
item.content:encoded:
PRAGMATA Floor It Training Simulation is a tricky simulation, much more so than the already challenging Medic Training and Mouse Trap simulations. While it is labeled as a combat training simulation, it is also a traversal challenge as you need to disengage locks and quickly alternate between jumping and shooting to complete all three objectives.

Table of contents

  • Simulation Objectives & Rewards
  • Complete Without Taking Damage
    • Step-by-Step Strategy

Simulation Objectives & Rewards

Complete Without Taking Damage

The most difficult objective for this training simulation is to complete it without taking damage. The Grapple Gun cannot damage bots, so you will need to rely on the Stasis Net Unit to prevent the flying bots from shooting at you and to stop the Executor in the middle section as you unlock the door via hacking.

To clear this objective, follow the strategy below

Step-by-Step Strategy

  1. Collect the Stasis Net at the start of the simulation.
  2. Shoot at the group of flying bots using the Stasis Net. This will also destroy the first storage box.
  3. Get on the platform and wait for it to move. Traverse to the second area.
  4. Here you will find two rotating laser hazards, an Executor and a locked door.
  5. Jump, and while in the air, hold L2/LT to start unlocking the first lock. Time will slow down while you are in the air, giving you more time to complete the sequence.
  6. Shoot the Stasis Net at the Executor to stop it. Jump and work on the second lock. Remember to destroy the storage box sitting between the two laser traps.
  7. Get through the door, pick the Stasis Net, and wait for the lasers to your right to appear.
  8. Follow them and shoot at the flying bot as you get closer with the Stasis Net. This will also destroy the storage box.
  9. This is the trickiest part. Jump on the first step of the massive stairs and shoot the Stasis Net at the two flying bots to your right.
  10. Jump to the second step and start aiming at the second group on the left. Once the Stasis Net has reloaded, shoot immediately.
  11. Rush towards the goal. When you see the platform with the final storage box, approach the goal, shoot at it to destroy the storage box.
  12. Get to the goal to complete the Training Simulation with all optional objectives.

This concludes our guide. For more help, visit our PRAGMATA Complete Walkthrough and Guide hub.

Guide based on a full playthrough completed at Standard difficulty in the PC version of the game. Screenshots captured from the same version

About the author: Francesco De Meo has been covering video games and technology since 2012, starting his career at small outlets like Gamersyndrome and GeekSnack. After joining Wccftech gaming section in 2015, he quickly expanded his video gaming coverage with in-depth reporting, interviews with iconic industry figures such as Grasshopper Manufacture founder and No More Heroes creator Goichi "Suda51" Suda, Resident Evil series creator Shinji Mikami, Team NINJA's president and Nioh series director Fumihiko Yasuda, and Silent Hill creator Keiichiro Toyama, reviews and on-the-ground coverage of major industry events such as Gamescom and E3. When he's not reporting or reviewing, Francesco can be found playing the genres he loves most, spending time with his six cats, reading, writing music, playing guitar and drumming for his progressive rock band.

item.title: PRAGMATA: Why You Should Always Finish Bots With a Critical Shot
item.content:encoded:
PRAGMATA features a few useful abilities with clear uses, and some whose benefits are not apparent. This is the case of Critical Shot, an early-game ability that can make your life much easier in some unexpected ways.

Table of contents

  • The Benefits of Finishing Enemies With a Critical Shot
  • How to Unlock Critical Shot
    • Using Critical Shot, Step-by-Step

The Benefits of Finishing Enemies With a Critical Shot

Finishing enemies with a Critical Shot, even without having it upgraded at level 3 or without having already collected certain mods that enhance the ability, is always worth it, for two reasons:

  • Every bot defeated with a Critical Shot drops extra Lunafilament. This makes it easier and faster to upgrade every weapon, ability, and hacking node to its maximum level.
  • During the animation, you are fully invincible, allowing you to ease off the pressure from enemies if you are surrounded by them.

How to Unlock Critical Shot

Critical Shot can be unlocked after completing Sector 01: Solar Power Plant. Access the Unit Printer in the Shelter, navigate to the Abilities Tab, and you will find the ability. It requires 1 Pure Lunum to unlock.

Using Critical Shot, Step-by-Step

  1. Damage the enemy until it is in critical health to make the Critical Shot hacking node appear on the Hacking Matrix.
  2. Navigate the Hacking Matrix and go through the node, successfully completing the hack to knock the enemy down.
  3. Perform the finishing blow by pressing the R2/RT/ZR button while the enemy is open.

This concludes our guide. For more help, visit our PRAGMATA Complete Walkthrough and Guide hub.

Guide based on a full playthrough completed at Standard difficulty in the PC version of the game. Screenshots captured from the same version

About the author: Francesco De Meo has been covering video games and technology since 2012, starting his career at small outlets like Gamersyndrome and GeekSnack. After joining Wccftech gaming section in 2015, he quickly expanded his video gaming coverage with in-depth reporting, interviews with iconic industry figures such as Grasshopper Manufacture founder and No More Heroes creator Goichi "Suda51" Suda, Resident Evil series creator Shinji Mikami, Team NINJA's president and Nioh series director Fumihiko Yasuda, and Silent Hill creator Keiichiro Toyama, reviews and on-the-ground coverage of major industry events such as Gamescom and E3. When he's not reporting or reviewing, Francesco can be found playing the genres he loves most, spending time with his six cats, reading, writing music, playing guitar and drumming for his progressive rock band.

Follow Wccftech on Google to get more of our news coverage in your feeds.



https://wccftech.com/how-to/pragmata-why-you-should-always-finish-bots-with-a-critical-shot/

item.title: PRAGMATA: Every Currency and How to Get It
item.content:encoded:
PRAGMATA features an intricate economy with multiple currencies centered around upgrading Hugh’s suit and weapons and Diana’s hacking capabilities. Understanding what these currencies are used for is essential for unlocking and upgrading everything you need as you make your way through the campaign.

Table of contents

  • Every Currency and Its Use
  • The Shelter Rewards Systems
    • Cabin's Stamp Club
    • Gifting REMs

Every Currency and Its Use

There are five currencies you will need to manage as you explore the lunar research station:

  • Lunafilament: The most common resource. It is used to unlock and enhance Attack Units, Tactical Units, and Defense Units. You will also need it for new Hacking Nodes, Hacking Modes, and certain character abilities.
  • Pure Lunum: A rarer resource. It is required to learn specific abilities and perform advanced enhancements for weapons.
  • Upgrade Components: Used specifically to upgrade your three basic parts: Suit, Primary Unit, and Hacking.
  • Cabin Coins: A currency used at the Shelter to unlock rewards via Cabin's Stamp Club.
  • Red Gate Keys: Consumable items used to unlock Red Gate areas. These are special combat challenge arenas that yield unique rewards, such as new mods.

Where to Find Them

The Shelter Rewards Systems

Cabin's Stamp Club

By interacting with Cabin in the Shelter, you can put your Cabin Coin to good use. By using coins, you can stamp squares on multiple Bingo Boards. Completing a line (Bingo) provides massive bonuses, new Hacking Nodes, weapons, and outfits for High and Diana.

Gifting REMs

REMs are 3D-printed toys and collectibles found throughout the research station. Gifting these to Diana in the Shelter unlocks some adorable interactions and makes you earn extra Cabin Coins for the Stamp Club.

This concludes our guide. For more help, visit our PRAGMATA Complete Walkthrough and Guide hub.

Guide based on a full playthrough completed at Standard difficulty in the PC version of the game. Screenshots captured from the same version

About the author: Francesco De Meo has been covering video games and technology since 2012, starting his career at small outlets like Gamersyndrome and GeekSnack. When he's not reporting or reviewing, Francesco can be found playing the genres he loves most, spending time with his six cats, reading, writing music, playing guitar and drumming for his progressive rock band.

Follow Wccftech on Google to get more of our news coverage in your feeds.



https://wccftech.com/how-to/pragmata-every-currency-and-how-to-get-it/
item.dc:creator: invalid@example.com (ResetEra_Newsbot

item.title: PRAGMATA: How Does Multihack Work? Master This Hacking Node For Ultimate Crowd Control
item.content:encoded:
PRAGMATA generally does a very good job of explaining its intricate mix of third-person shooting and hacking puzzle-solving, but not every mechanic is explained as thoroughly as it should be.

This is the case of the Multihack hacking node, one of the best hacking abilities in the entire game, which can turn the most difficult combat encounters into a walk in the park with the right setup.

Table of contents

  • Multihack: The Best Tool For Crowd Control
  • Powerful Multihack Combos

According to the in-game description, Multihack is a "hacking node that links enemies together, opening multiple targets. OPEN time is extended while damage to affected targets is reduced. Passing through multiple nodes of the same type extends OPEN duration."

Going by this, this seems like an average tradeoff, as reduced damage is inevitably going to increase your time in combat against multiple targets. However, there's a hidden mechanic the game doesn't tell you about that makes Multihack the most powerful hacking tool in the game: it also applies the effects of every other hacking node you activate!

Powerful Multihack Combos

When you get the chance to unlock Multihack a few hours into the game, the only other hacking node available to you is Decode, which increases damage dealt to affected enemies, essentially offsetting Multihack's reduced damage effect. However, as soon as you unlock other nodes like Confuse and Freeze, you can put some very effective combos to good use.

  • Multihack + Confuse: With this combo (which is the one to use to clear the Bad Blood Training Simulation), you can have multiple enemies fight one another, taking the heat off you and letting you pick them off one by one via either shooting and consecutive hacking with Offense Mode and the Hack Reset Secret Technique.
  • Multihack + Freeze + Decoy Generator Defense Unit: With this combo, you not only get to stun multiple enemies at the same time, but you can also get them to where you want them to be by releasing a decoy with the Decoy Generator. This will let you use weapons that can hit multiple enemies at once, like the Charge Piercer, to damage a larger number of enemies at once. The best early Loadout Synergy in the game is also more effective with this combo, extending the enemies' stun time for even more massive damage.

As fights against multiple bots at the same time are the most challenging in the game, learning how to leverage these combos will turn bigger enemy numbers into your advantage.

This concludes our guide. For more help, visit our PRAGMATA Complete Walkthrough and Guide hub.

Guide based on a full playthrough completed at Standard difficulty in the PC version of the game. Screenshots captured from the same version

About the author: Francesco De Meo has been covering video games and technology since 2012, starting his career at small outlets like Gamersyndrome and GeekSnack. After joining Wccftech gaming section in 2015, he quickly expanded his video gaming coverage with in-depth reporting, interviews with iconic industry figures such as Grasshopper Manufacture founder and No More Heroes creator Goichi "Suda51" Suda, Resident Evil series creator Shinji Mikami, Team NINJA's president and Nioh series director Fumihiko Yasuda, and Silent Hill creator Keiichiro Toyama, reviews and on-the-ground coverage of major industry events such as Gamescom and E3. When he's not reporting or reviewing, Francesco can be found playing the genres he loves most, spending time with his six cats, reading, writing music, playing guitar and drumming for his progressive rock band.

Follow Wccftech on Google to get more of our news coverage in your feeds.



https://wccftech.com/how-to/pragmata-how-does-multihack-work-master-this-hacking-node-for-ultimate-crowd-control/
item.dc:creator: invalid@example.com (ResetEra_Newsbot

item.title: T1 Threatens Legal Action over Player Harassment and Privacy Violations
item.content:encoded:
T1 has issued statements that threaten “formal legal action” over online harassment of its players and violations of player privacy. The organization asked for fans’ cooperation in “fostering a healthy and respectful fan culture.”

The statements follow the legal action brought by the player agency, Fanable, who represent esports players, including Lee ‘Faker’ Sang-hyeok and Lee ‘Gumayusi’ Min-hyeong.

T1 statements issue a stern warning over player harassment

T1 issued two statements over social media, warning of legal action against different types of harassment, with the first statement focusing on player privacy.

The Korean organization stated, “Recently, there have been numerous recorded instances of privacy infringement against our players, including waiting outside their team house for unauthorized filming as they head to our HQ.”

T1’s headquarters has in the past reportedly been the target of protesting fans, including hired trucks to block the building’s access, with staff and players reportedly receiving funeral wreaths.

The organization added that actions that take place outside of the players’ working schedule “cannot be justified under any circumstances.”

The statement then threatens legal action, stating, “Should these violations continue, we will take firm measures, including legal action, to ensure the safety and protect the rights of our players.”

The organization asked, “for the cooperation of all fans in fostering a healthy and respectful fan culture.”

A second statement, the following day, targeted online harassment, stating, “We are closely monitoring online communities and social media platforms for the creation of malicious rumors, the dissemination of false information, and posts containing personal attacks, defamation, or mockery directed at our players.”

Adding, “Actions that damage the reputation of our players and cause psychological distress may result in civil and criminal liability under relevant laws and regulations… In cases where malicious behavior is repetitive or severe, we intend to respond strictly, which may include taking formal legal action.”

T1 statements follow Faker and Gumayusi legal action

The statements closely follow news from Korea that the agency Fanable would take legal action to protect its athletes from online harassment.

The agency stated, “The creation, distribution, and defamation of malicious posts regarding our athletes in some communities have continued at a level that exceeds acceptable limits without any effort at self-regulation.”

Adding, “We have reached the conclusion that we can no longer overlook this situation,” before confirming both civil and criminal action.

Gumayusi was the victim of high-profile harassment last year, with Korean lawmaker Jeon Yong-gi calling for more robust action by Korea e-Sports Association (KeSPA), Riot Games, and orgs to tackle online abuse.

The lawmaker directly attacked T1 fan group T1 Support Gallery (aka T1 Gall), stating, “I deeply regret the behaviour of some community users, such as the ‘T1 Support Gallery’, who have been cyberbullying [Gumayusi] for a long time, and I urge them to stop immediately.” (translated)

Does more need to be done to protect players? Discuss that on our Discord.
For more League of Legends news, see why Sheo suddenly departed Team Heretics.

Darragh is an Esports Journalist for Insider Gaming specialising in Counter-Strike. He loves to explore how esports teams work, or why they very often do not.

More from Darragh Harbinson

MOBILE APP

Level Up Your Gaming News

Real-time news, exclusive podcasts, push alerts, and a better reading experience.

Available on iOS & Android



https://insider-gaming.com/t1-threatens-legal-action-harassment-privacy/
item.dc:creator: invalid@example.com (ResetEra_Newsbot

item.title: NYT Connections Hints Today (#1040)—Answers for Thursday, April 16
item.content:encoded:
How’s that NYT Connections streak looking? Are you managing a perfect record throughout this week? If not, let’s right that wrong, and if you are, then let’s keep pushing! I’ve got today’s NYT Connections hints and answers in this guide, so you can get the solution in place as quickly as possible.

Scroll down to get the NYT Connections help you might be looking for!

How to Play NYT Connections

In June 2023, NYT Connections emerged as yet another game in the ever-growing lineup of the New York Times titles. This title soared immediately, and it’s easy to see why. As a free-to-play, accessible New York Times game, NYT Connections is available worldwide and can be played on desktop devices or in a mobile app.

But how do you play NYT Connections?

The NYT team will provide you with a grid of 16 words once a day. The only task left up to you is to sort those words into four groups of four by finding their commonalities. Every NYT Connections puzzle has just one solution, and some words can have double meanings, so it can sometimes be tricky to figure out the answers.

It’s also important to stress that the words are color-coded: yellow, blue, green, and purple. That’s in order of difficulty, from least to most.

What’s critical here is that you get four guesses every day, and if you use those, your NYT Connections answers for today will be out of reach, as it’ll be game over.

Today’s NYT Connections Hints (April 16)

Here are the words from today’s NYT Connections puzzle:

  • COOL
  • WHIP
  • MASK
  • RIDE
  • NEEDLE
  • CAMP
  • FAN
  • WHEELS
  • AUTO
  • BODYSUIT
  • HEAT
  • ROAST
  • DAY
  • CLAWS
  • BRA
  • RIB

And here are the hints I can offer for today’s NYT Connections:

  • Yellow – Words you can use in place of ‘tease’
  • Green – Words you might find on a thermostat
  • Blue – Items you’d find in Catwoman’s closet
  • Purple – Terms that follow ‘training’

Here’s the final warning before we get to the NYT Connections answers for today, April 16:

Are you sure you want to scroll further?

Very well!

NYT Connections Answers Explained for Today, April 16 (#1040)

If you want to jump straight to the answers for today’s NYT Connections puzzle, I’ve got you covered:

  • Yellow – NEEDLE, RIB, RIDE, ROAST
  • Green – AUTO, COOL, FAN, HEAT
  • Blue – BODYSUIT, CLAWS, MASK, WHIP
  • Purple – BRA, CAMP, DAY, WHEELS

When Does NYT Connections Reset?

That’s today’s puzzle, but if you want the next one, the game resets daily at midnight local time (that’s your local time). You’ll have 24 hours to complete the puzzle when midnight ticks over in your timezone.

How did you do? Did you get a perfect run? Let me know what your streak looks like on the Insider Gaming Discord server.

Senior Editor and Esports Lead

Grant has been gaming for 30+ years and in the industry for 10+. You'll probably find him playing a post-apocalyptic game or an extraction shooter somewhere.

More from Grant Taylor-Hill

MOBILE APP

Level Up Your Gaming News

Real-time news, exclusive podcasts, push alerts, and a better reading experience.

Available on iOS & Android



https://insider-gaming.com/nyt-connections-hints-solutions-april-16-1040/
item.dc:creator: invalid@example.com (ResetEra_Newsbot

item.title: Beast of Reincarnation Pre-Order Details and Game Editions Revealed
item.content:encoded:
A reliable insider has stated that Beast of Reincarnation pre-orders should begin today and has revealed pricing, Deluxe edition content, and more.

For those unaware, the developers behind the iconic Pokémon games, Game Freak, announced that they were venturing into a brand new realm with their new title, Beast of Reincarnation. The developers recently announced that the souls-like RPG should release this August, and it seems players can begin pre-ordering the game today.

Beast of Reincarnation Pre-Orders ‘Should’ Begin Today

Reliable insider billbil-kun recently took to X (formerly Twitter) to say that Beast of Reincarnation should kick off today, April 16. The US pricing for the title was also revealed, with the standard edition costing $59.99 and the deluxe edition priced at $69.99. Players who pre-order the title will receive the Brown Shiba Skin for Koo and 30,000 Amber, the in-game currency.

Coming to the deluxe edition content, the post reveals that it will feature Black & Brown Shiba Skins for Koo, “Oni’s Hat” for Emma, Emma’s sword “Big Dipper”, 100,000 Amber (in-game currency), and several vegetable seedlings. The title will also receive a physical release, with PS5 users receiving physical copies of both the standard and deluxe editions. In contrast, Xbox users only get the option for the standard edition.

“Beast of Reincarnation” is set in a post-apocalyptic Japan, offering players a unique “one-person, one-dog action RPG” experience. The story follows Emma, an outcast shunned by society because of her affliction, and her companion, Koo, a blighted dog. Players will journey through this world, afflicted by blight, with Koo by their side, encountering various challenges and uncovering dangerous truths. Along the way, they will face formidable bosses whose powers they must capture.

In other news, Kota Furushima recently addressed long-standing Game Freak criticism while discussing Beast of Reincarnation. What are your thoughts on Beast of Reincarnation pre-order and game edition details? Leave your thoughts down in the comments, and join the official Insider Gaming Discord server.

For more information from Insider Gaming, read about Windrose hitting 500,000 players within 2 days of early access. Don’t forget to sign up for our weekly newsletter.

MOBILE APP

Level Up Your Gaming News

Real-time news, exclusive podcasts, push alerts, and a better reading experience.

Available on iOS & Android



https://insider-gaming.com/beast-of-reincarnation-pre-order-details-and-game-editions-revealed/

item.title: Windrose Hits 500,000 Players As Positive Reviews Soar in Early Access
item.content:encoded:
The team at Kraken Express has revealed how Windrose, the open-world pirate survival game, has managed to hit half a million players in just two days of early access. The game dropped on April 14, emerging to strong reviews on account of all the meat on the game’s bones, the charming visuals, and the massive open-world environment ripe for exploration.

Windrose was onto something solid in the earliest days of its playtests, and the recent demo that went live during Steam Next Fest was one of the most popular on the entire platform.

Windrose Sets Sail and Dominates the Seas

The pirating space has been dipped into aplenty over the years, with games like Black Flag, Sea of Thieves, Greedfall, and Skull & Bones all offering their take on an open-ocean, pirate theme. Windrose has taken the concept and turned it into a PvE survival title with heaps of customization, construction, and intense combat sequences.

Windrose, formerly known as Crosswind, gives players a vast world to dig into right out of the gate. Players are thrown into the deep end after suffering a shipwreck, and it’s up to them to rebuild their pirating empire, starting with little more than sticks and stones.

Before long, you’ll find yourself at the helm of a hulking ship, mastering a crew, outposts, and troubling anyone who challenges you on the open seas. There isn’t any PvP in Windrose yet, but it’s early days, so watch this space.

In a post on social media, Kraken Express revealed that a whopping half a million players have entered Windrose in just two days of early access:

What makes this more impressive is that Windrose isn’t just another free-to-play survival title. It has a fairly decent price tag of around $26.99, which means Kraken Express has already made bank. The studio, backed by Pocketpair Publishing, hasn’t made anything as a collective before Windrose, making the success even more admirable.

On Steam, Windrose has managed to generate a peak concurrent player count of more than 97,000 users, and the slew of reviews stitched together by users is reflecting in 85% positivity, which is nuts for a brand-new title in the early access space.

Have you played Windrose? Let us know if you’re enjoying it on the Insider Gaming Discord server.

For more Insider Gaming coverage, check out the news that PIONER has a new 2026 roadmap

Senior Editor and Esports Lead

Grant has been gaming for 30+ years and in the industry for 10+. You'll probably find him playing a post-apocalyptic game or an extraction shooter somewhere.

More from Grant Taylor-Hill

MOBILE APP

Level Up Your Gaming News

Real-time news, exclusive podcasts, push alerts, and a better reading experience.

Available on iOS & Android



https://insider-gaming.com/windrose-hits-500000-players-positive-reviews-soar-early-access/

item.title: Cyberpunk 2077's funkiest vehicle modder has resurrected a cut police gunship from the RPG's very first trailer
item.content:encoded:
They've also added in a short quest to unlock it

As you may have gathered by this point, I'm a sucker for a cool Cyberpunk 2077 mod. Hence, when I saw that the modder behind some of the RPG's most unique custom vehicles had dug up some of CD Projekt's abandoned models to bring to life a flying police interceptor that never made it into full game, you best believe I stuck that on my must cover list. It's called an and they've also put together a short quest to unlock it.

This weaponised gunship, which modder Oranje3's dubbed the NCPD Night Hunter, showed up centre stage in the original teaser trailer CD Projekt put out for Cyberpunk all the way back in 2013 (embedded above). At that time, the RPG looked like it might be going all in Bladerunner-esque flying transports, rather than simply having a few airbourne craft streak between the skyscrapers as the majority of folks commute by regular roads below. Naturally, once the game was out, modders were quick to stick some thrusters on its ground-dwelling rides.

While the Night Hunter's far from Cyberpunk's first flying ride mod, I'd argue it's the coolest yet. First of all, it isn't reliant on established car flight mods, and second it's tricked out with a bunch of custom features to help make it feel distinct. It's got its own HUD, animations, and an array of weapons that includes front-mounted machine guns, a missile launcher, and EMP countermeasures. If that's not enough, it'll set people on fire if you hover over them. Sure, this can also result in any passengers who sit in the back of it also catching fire, but that's a necessary sacrifice.

To start the quest to unlock it, you'll need to head out to the abandoned town of Rocky Ridge in the Badlands, there, you'll get a text from a bloke called OJ and need to head on a shard treasure hunt across Night City. Once that's done, the Night Hunter won't be summonable via the car menu, but instead always spawns at various points around the map.

Honestly, I find it bonkers that Cyberpunk modders are still seemingly unearthing stuff in its files they can turn into working mods. Though, it sounds like Oranje3's had to put in plenty of work here, so the AV wasn't just waiting in the wings 90% finished. Nothing a modder who's previously turned Johnny Silverhand's Porsche into a tricycle and made a wacky motorbike with one big wheel couldn't handle, through.

Because of the quest, if you fancy giving this mod a go, you'll need to grab the following: Cyber Engine Tweaks, RED4ext, ArchiveXL, TweakXL, and Redscript. The Phantom Liberty DLC's also a must-have.



https://www.rockpapershotgun.com/cyberpunk-2077s-funkiest-vehicle-modder-has-resurrected-a-cut-police-gunship-from-the-rpgs-very-first-trailer
item.dc:creator: invalid@example.com (ResetEra_Newsbot

item.title: Fortnite v40.20 April 16 Update—Complete Patch Notes
item.content:encoded:
If you’ve come for all the Fortnite v40.20 patch notes in the April 16 update, you’re in the right place. We’ve got news of gameplay fixes, visual improvements, and changes to the Battle Pass and Quests.

As with every week, everyone was waiting for the Fortnite Servers to be back up after the April 16 downtime. While you were doing this, Epic was making some adjustments to the game, which should improve the experience overall.

All Fortnite v40.20 Patch Notes For April 16 Update

The initial patch notes were leaked courtesy of FireMonkey, and here’s what has been reported.

“GENERAL FIXES & IMPROVEMENTS

This update comes with a large number of bug fixes across all of Fortnite. Here are a few notable ones we’ve seen reported in community spaces:

Gameplay

  • Chaos Reloader Shotgun now reloads correctly while unequipped, displays reload progress in the Quick Bar, and fires consistently. 
  • Thorne’s Vampiric Blade no longer causes the jump input to become unresponsive after using its secondary attack.
  • Port-a-Bunkers and Shield Bubbles are now available in Ranked Zero Build as intended.
  • Your arrow will no longer incorrectly appear below teammates’ arrows on the minimap.
  • Returning to Fortnite after Alt-Tabbing or pressing the Windows key no longer incorrectly opens your inventory when in a match. 

Cosmetics & Visuals

  • Fixed an issue causing some Outfits and cosmetics to appear shinier or darker than intended on higher quality settings.
  • Fixed visual and animation physics issues affecting certain Outfits on Performance Mode or lower-end devices.
  • The Saitama Outfit’s cape now loads its textures correctly.
  • The Bugs Bunny Outfit’s eyes no longer appear broken when previewing Pickaxes and Back Blings in the Locker.
  • Darkfire Bundle Weapon Wraps now display their correct individual glow colors instead of all appearing purple.
  • Fixed an issue where some players’ profiles could incorrectly display the default Outfit.
  • Cosmetic previews now display cleanly without a persisting loading spinner in the Shop and Locker.

Battle Pass & Quests

  • Fixed an issue where the Battle Pass could show an incorrect unlock notification when previewing a different reward set. 
  • Daily Quests now display the correct refresh time and no longer reset earlier than stated.

Fortnite OG

  • Item icons no longer appear stretched on Vending Machines.
  • Players can now manage their Quick Bar inventory without issues after using a Pirate Cannon.
  • The Reboot Van at Paradise Palms is no longer missing.
  • Daily Quests no longer reference locations that have been removed from the current map rotation.

Other Modes & Platforms

  • [Reload] Ranked matches no longer display the “Placeholder Playlist” text on the Starter Island.
  • [LEGO Fortnite] Fixed an issue where the World Restore option would give an error when attempting to restore to a previous checkpoint.
  • [Mobile] Fixed visual issues affecting gameplay for some players on Google Pixel 10 devices.
  • [Mobile] The Emote Wheel is no longer too small and difficult to read on certain devices.
  • For Save the World fixes and improvements, check out this Communities Post.

We’re continuing to monitor your reports on social media and in community spaces, please keep them coming and we’ll work to fix them.”

What do you make of the newest Fortnite update? Is there anything Epic keeps overlooking each week? Let us know your gripes and feedback through the Insider Gaming Discord.

We have much more Fortnite news here, including word on a Fortnite & The Boys coming together for a heroic collaboration, and Fortnite adding AI-Powered NPCs.

Written by

Evergreen Editor

Andy is Insider Gaming's Senior Evergreen Editor and has been in the games journalism sphere in one way or another for over 7 years. His video game taste is as…

More from Andrew Highton

MOBILE APP

Level Up Your Gaming News

Real-time news, exclusive podcasts, push alerts, and a better reading experience.

Available on iOS & Android



https://insider-gaming.com/fortnite-v40-20-april-16-update-patch-notes/
item.dc:creator: invalid@example.com (ResetEra_Newsbot

item.title: Starfield Nintendo Switch 2 Spotted
item.content:encoded:
A version of Starfield for Nintendo Switch 2 has been leaked by the Taiwan ratings board, suggesting Bethesda's space RPG will land on the console.

As spotted by Universonintendo, the listing shows a Switch 2 version of the game being approved for release just this week, though there's no mention yet of a formal release date.

Starfield just touched down on PlayStation 5, nearly three years after its original arrival on PC and Xbox Series X/S. Billed as the next big franchise from the makers of The Elder Scrolls and Fallout, the space-based role-player has received a more muted response.

Bethesda has since launched various updates to add fan-requested features, though recently played down the game's latest big update as not being a "2.0" version of the game. "I think it’s the kind of thing where if you love Starfield, we think you’re going to love this," Bethesda development chief Todd Howard said last month. "It’s updates and things that change the game, not in an isolated way, but sort of meta."

Recent months have brought rumors of a Switch 2 version being in the works — though Bethesda itself has not confirmed anything publicly. With technical issues currently affecting players on PS5, Bethesda will likely want to ensure that whenever Starfield does finally hit Switch 2, everything is running smoothly.

Of course, Microsoft has published various other former console exclusives on Switch previously — such as Grounded and Pentiment. Bethesda's Indiana Jones and the Great Circle launches on Switch 2 next month, while Oblivion Remastered is also set for release later this year.

Back in 2023, Howard discussed the benefits of Starfield being an Xbox exclusive at the time, namechecking the brand association Nintendo enjoys with its first-party games. "I do also think people attach brands to certain games," he said. "When you think of Zelda you think of the Switch and I think there are times when that can be a real benefit."

Tom Phillips is IGN's News Editor. You can reach Tom at tom_phillips@ign.com or find him on Bluesky @tomphillipseg.bsky.social



https://www.ign.com/articles/starfield-nintendo-switch-2-spotted
item.dc:creator: invalid@example.com (ResetEra_Newsbot

item.title: Sunsoft's Upcoming Switch Game Has One Of The Most Ridiculous Titles Ever
item.content:encoded:
Though this won't be new to light novel fans

Publisher Sunsoft is about to release a light novel game adaptation on 23rd April 2026, and it's honestly got one of the best, most ridiculous titles we've ever seen.

Given that it is an adaptation, those familiar with the original work likely won't be surprised, but we're just tickled by the fact that an already-lengthy title has been extended even more for the upcoming game.

So, are you ready..? Here we go:

GAME The Strongest Job Is Apparently Not a Hero or a Sage, but an Appraiser (Provisional)!~Dungeon and Mystery Girl~

So for those wondering, the original title of the light novel series is The Strongest Job Is Apparently Not a Hero or a Sage, but an Appraiser (Provisional)!, so you can see where it's been extended for the game. The light novel series has also been adapted into a manga series, with an anime TV series also now streaming over on Crunchyroll.

Granted, it's not quite up there with Mon-Yu: Defeat Monsters And Gain Strong Weapons And Armor. You May Be Defeated, But Don't Give Up. Become Stronger. I Believe There Will Be A Day When The Heroes Defeat The Devil King, but it's not too far off.

We love it though, and the game itself looks like it might provide some decent top-down action for fans of the series, so maybe one to keep an eye on, hm?

What do you make of GAME The Strongest Job Is Apparently... oh, you know. Let us know your thoughts with a comment in the usual place.

[source nintendo.com]

Nintendo Life’s resident horror fanatic, when he’s not knee-deep in Resident Evil and Silent Hill lore, Ollie likes to dive into a good horror book while nursing a lovely cup of tea. He also enjoys long walks and listens to everything from TOOL to Chuck Berry.

  • Author Profile
  • Bluesky
  • Reply


https://www.nintendolife.com/news/2026/04/sunsofts-upcoming-switch-game-has-one-of-the-most-ridiculous-titles-ever
item.dc:creator: invalid@example.com (ResetEra_Newsbot

item.title: ‘It’s A Delight to Constantly Beat Impossible Memory Constraints’: No Man’s Sky Dev Reveals 3x Optimization Work For Switch 2 and Steam Deck
item.content:encoded:
Developing for platforms like the Nintendo Switch, Nintendo Switch 2, and Steam Deck can be a challenge, especially for developers who have to work around the hardware restraints these systems have, compared to consoles like the PlayStation 5 and Xbox Series X. However, some developers like No Man's Sky developer Hello Games are more than willing to put in the extra work to keep their games updated and work around “impossible memory constraints.”

“The mobile platforms like Switch 1 and 2, along with Steam Deck take a disproportionate amount of engineering time with every update we release,” said No Man's Sky Engine Programmer Martin Griffiths on X. “A bunch of us at Hello Games probably spend 2-3x more time to make these updates seamlessly work, exactly like the other consoles do along with PC/Mac etc.”

The biggest hardware constraint developers have to work around to continue supporting growing games on these platforms is, as mentioned above, memory constraints. “It’s really a delight to constantly beat impossible memory constraints with a growing game to allow people to continue to experience and enjoy No Man's Sky,” the developer concluded.

Memory constraints in gaming systems have long been known as the first hurdle most developers face. Even after the launch of the Nintendo Switch 2, the Xbox Series S, with its 10 GB of RAM, remains the current-generation gaming system with the least available RAM, which continues to create challenges for developers. For example, the system's low available RAM, alongside the parity clause, was among the reasons Baldur's Gate 3 took so long to launch on Xbox systems, as the system couldn't run the game's split-screen multiplayer mode properly.

About the author: Francesco De Meo has been covering video games and technology since 2012, starting his career at small outlets like Gamersyndrome and GeekSnack. After joining Wccftech gaming section in 2015, he quickly expanded his video gaming coverage with in-depth reporting, interviews with iconic industry figures such as Grasshopper Manufacture founder and No More Heroes creator Goichi "Suda51" Suda, Resident Evil series creator Shinji Mikami, Team NINJA's president and Nioh series director Fumihiko Yasuda, and Silent Hill creator Keiichiro Toyama, reviews and on-the-ground coverage of major industry events such as Gamescom and E3. When he's not reporting or reviewing, Francesco can be found playing the genres he loves most, spending time with his six cats, reading, writing music, playing guitar and drumming for his progressive rock band.

Follow Wccftech on Google to get more of our news coverage in your feeds.



https://wccftech.com/switch-2-3x-optimization-work-no-mans-sky/
item.dc:creator: invalid@example.com (ResetEra_Newsbot
